steps.title: Compare the two texts
steps.content: Text 1 presents evidence that predators can control prey numbers. Text 2 accepts that many studies show this pattern, but explains why it may not apply to large, slowly reproducing prey such as Dall sheep.

steps.title: Reject unsupported limits
steps.content: Choice B says contrary findings have not been accepted, which Text 2 does not claim. Choice C shifts the limitation to predator traits, but Text 2 focuses on prey traits. Choice D invents methodological flaws in the supporting studies. The remaining choice captures both parts of Text 2's stance: the principle has support, but it should not be treated as universal. The answer is A.
